headline: Oil Prices Slump, Exxon and Chevron Stocks Drop After Trump Iran Deal
summary: Oil prices slumped to three-month lows of just above $80 a barrel early Monday after the U.S. and Iran reached an interim deal that includes plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Exxon Mobil Chevron  and other energy stocks were among the sharpest fallers in the S&P 500 ahead of the open as the broader market looked set to rally.  Energy infrastructure company  Sempra  was down 4.7%, while  Occidental Petroleum ConocoPhillips  and  Exxon  were all around 3% lower.

content.title: Oil Prices Slump, Exxon and Chevron Stocks Drop After Trump Iran Deal
content.summary: Oil prices slumped to three-month lows of just above $80 a barrel early Monday after the U.S. and Iran reached an interim deal that includes plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Exxon Mobil Chevron  and other energy stocks were among the sharpest fallers in the S&P 500 ahead of the open as the broader market looked set to rally.  Energy infrastructure company  Sempra  was down 4.7%, while  Occidental Petroleum ConocoPhillips  and  Exxon  were all around 3% lower.

headline: Today’s AI market hits all 5 warning signs that preceded 2000 Nasdaq crash.
summary: Although Wall Street has spent the better part of two years cheering an artificial intelligence buildout that has dragged the Nasdaq 100 up roughly 30% over the past year, the technology-packed Nasdaq Composite is starting to wobble at the worst possible moment. The benchmark S&P 500 has slipped, with SPY off about 4% in the ... Today’s AI market hits all 5 warning signs that preceded 2000 Nasdaq crash.

headline: Oil Prices Slump, Exxon and Valero Stocks Drop After Trump Iran Deal
summary: Oil prices slumped to the lowest level since early March of around $80 a barrel Monday after the U.S. and Iran reached an interim deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the U.S. naval blockade.  Valero Marathon Petroleum Phillips 66 Exxon Mobil  and other energy stocks were among the sharpest decliners in the  Monday as the broader market rallied amid optimism to an end to the conflict that began on Feb. 28 when the U.S. launched a military strike on Iran.  President Donald Trump said “the deal is now complete” in a social media post Sunday.

content.title: Oil Prices Slump, Exxon and Valero Stocks Drop After Trump Iran Deal
content.summary: Oil prices slumped to the lowest level since early March of around $80 a barrel Monday after the U.S. and Iran reached an interim deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the U.S. naval blockade.  Valero Marathon Petroleum Phillips 66 Exxon Mobil  and other energy stocks were among the sharpest decliners in the  Monday as the broader market rallied amid optimism to an end to the conflict that began on Feb. 28 when the U.S. launched a military strike on Iran.  President Donald Trump said “the deal is now complete” in a social media post Sunday.

headline: Oil Prices Dive On U.S.-Iran Deal, But Prewar Pricing Is Far Off
summary: Oil prices plunged after the U.S. and Iran agreed Sunday on a framework to end the war.  The agreement cleared a path for a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days.  A host of geopolitical risks and supply challenges could keep oil prices elevated.

content.title: Oil Prices Dive On U.S.-Iran Deal, But Prewar Pricing Is Far Off
content.summary: Oil prices plunged after the U.S. and Iran agreed Sunday on a framework to end the war.  The agreement cleared a path for a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days.  A host of geopolitical risks and supply challenges could keep oil prices elevated.

headline: Will Strong Oil Prices Boost ExxonMobil's Energy Business?
summary: XOM's low-cost assets in the Permian Basin and Guyana position it to capitalize on elevated crude prices and a favorable 2026 oil market outlook.

headline: Chip stocks surge, oil slides closer to $80 on US-Iran deal, Fox to acquire Roku
summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma comes on Market Domination to further discuss today's biggest headlines, including memory chip stocks like Micron Technology (MU) surging as Wall Street leans more bullish on the AI trade, oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) moving closer to $80 per barrel on news of a US-Iran peace deal, and media giant Fox (FOX, FOXA) striking a deal to acquire Roku (ROKU) for $22 billion.
